description: account set-regular-key command — assign or remove a regular signing key on an XRPL account

userStories.title: account set-regular-key command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to assign or remove a regular signing key on my account from the CLI so I can sign future transactions without exposing my master key.
acceptanceCriteria: Read SetRegularKey docs at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl-dev-portal/blob/master/docs/references/protocol/transactions/types/setregularkey.md before implementing
Create src/commands/account/set-regular-key.ts and register it in src/commands/account/index.ts
xrpl account set-regular-key subcommand with: --key address (optional, base58 address of new regular key), --remove flag (removes existing regular key by omitting RegularKey field from tx)
CLI exits with error if both --key and --remove are provided
CLI exits with error if neither --key nor --remove is provided
When --remove is used, RegularKey field is omitted entirely from the transaction (not set to empty)
tests/e2e/account/account.set-regular-key.validation.test.ts: both flags together errors, neither flag errors, missing key material errors (no network)
tests/e2e/account/account.set-regular-key.test.ts: set a regular key and verify via account info; remove the regular key and verify it is cleared via account info; --json, --dry-run
